chief_complaint: A 24-year-old female triathlete presents with amenorrhea and a history of bulimia nervosa.
history: The patient has a 12-year history of bulimia nervosa and has been experiencing hypothalamic amenorrhea since age 21. She has a training volume of approximately 20 hours per week and a critically low energy availability (< 10 kcal/fat free mass/day).
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination: not provided.
labs: DXA scan: lower than expected BMD at lumbar spine Z-score -2.3 SD. HR-pQCT: lower cortical and trabecular vBMD and trabecular number compared to normative data for young Brazilian women.
correct_diagnosis: Spontaneous recovery of the Female Athlete Triad during the COVID-19 pandemic
differentials: Spontaneous recovery of the Female Athlete Triad during the COVID-19 pandemic
correct_ranking: Spontaneous recovery of the Female Athlete Triad during the COVID-19 pandemic
